briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of embedded banking middleware provider Synapse, highlighting systemic risks in fintech infrastructure.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are shifting to a domestically controlled sovereign payment network, reducing reliance on US card giants Visa and Mastercard.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competitive dynamics in the payments industry.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple's acquisition of UK open banking startup Credit Kudos signals the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and alternative credit decisioning.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Hot bet summer? Soon Canadians will be able to trade on prediction markets](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/prediction-markets-wealthsimple-9.7239575?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Wealthsimple and others are preparing to offer Canadians access to prediction markets, opening a new frontier in retail financial products.

- **[Questrade launches custom indexing as massive IPOs reshape the market](https://betakit.com/questrade-launches-custom-indexing-as-massive-ipos-reshape-the-market/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian investment platform Questrade has introduced custom indexing, giving retail investors more personalized portfolio control amid a wave of major IPO activity.

- **[Bell and Canadian AI firm Cohere sign deal to operate models using Bell infrastructure](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bell-cohere-ai-infrastructure-deal-9.7240577?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Bell and Toronto-based AI company Cohere have partnered to run large language models on Canadian telecommunications infrastructure, advancing domestic AI capacity.

- **[Canada bans "sophisticated deepfakes" of political figures](https://betakit.com/canada-bans-sophisticated-deepfakes-of-political-figures/)** — *BetaKit* — The Canadian government has moved to prohibit AI-generated deepfakes of political figures, with implications for trust, identity verification, and fraud prevention in digital financial services.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — *(Relevant background context)* Canada's formal stance against screen scraping and its push toward a regulated open banking framework continues to shape the country's fintech regulatory trajectory.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A thought-provoking resource cataloguing the darker consequences of fintech products gone wrong — essential reading for ecosystem builders prioritizing responsible and inclusive financial innovation in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Cybersecurity coding bootcamp aims to help Canadian veterans transition to tech jobs](https://betakit.com/cybersecurity-coding-bootcamp-aims-to-help-canadian-veterans-transition-to-tech-jobs/)** — *BetaKit* — A new Canadian cybersecurity bootcamp targeting veterans speaks to the broader talent pipeline and workforce inclusion challenges relevant to growing fintech and tech ecosystems in Atlantic Canada.
